finding: After submitting the application the only confirmation is a small line of green text beside the button; the form does not clear, disable, or redirect, so a non-technical user cannot tell the submission succeeded and will resubmit.

suggested_fix.current: Small inline green text 'Thanks. We received your details and will follow up shortly.' next to the SUBMIT button; form stays filled, no redirect
suggested_fix.proposed: On success, redirect to the existing /thank-you-for-applying page (or show a full-width banner above the form), and clear/disable the form

finding: The Blog/News CMS has no entry in the studio sidebar; the entire content type is only reachable by typing /admin/news.

suggested_fix.current: Sidebar 'Admin sections' = SEO pages, Media library only
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a 'Blog & News' link (→ /admin/news) to the studio sidebar 'Admin sections' nav

finding: No phone number or email address anywhere on the homepage or in the footer — every contact path routes into a web form, leaving a frustrated user with no way to reach a human.

suggested_fix.current: Footer and site offer only nav links and a contact form; no phone/email
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a visible support email (e.g. support@vendingpreneurs.com) and/or phone number to the footer and contact page, plus a stated response time

finding: Homepage (and /case-studies) testimonial videos each produce keyboard tab stops with no visible focus indicator, and the crawler flags a possible focus trap at the last stop of each of the four video groups; the missing focus indicator is the confirmed defect, the trap is a heuristic flag needing manual keyboard confirmation.

suggested_fix.current: Custom video players with no managed focus and no visible focus ring
suggested_fix.proposed: Ensure each video control is reachable and escapable by keyboard (no trap), and add a visible :focus-visible outline (e.g. 2px solid high-contrast ring) on the player and its controls

finding: Homepage hero stats default to '0+ / $0M+ / 0+' for no-JS users, crawlers, and anyone whose scroll/IntersectionObserver does not fire; real values (500+, $3M+, 3,000+) only appear via a client-side count-up triggered on scroll into view.

suggested_fix.current: 0+ Entrepreneurs launched / $0M+ Snack-drink sales / 0+ Vending locations
suggested_fix.proposed: Populate real verified figures (e.g. '600+ entrepreneurs launched', '$12M+ in machine sales', '2,000+ locations'); if the count-up animation is failing, render the final value server-side as a fallback so it never shows 0

finding: Three required fields are commitments I may not have made yet: Business stage, Available startup budget, and Launch timeline are all required

suggested_fix.current: Business stage, budget, and timeline marked '(required)'
suggested_fix.proposed: Make only Name + Email required; mark stage/budget/timeline optional with a 'Not sure yet' option in each dropdown

finding: The navigation item and route say 'NEWS' but the page heading is 'BLOG', and article breadcrumbs also say 'BLOG' — the same section has two different names.

suggested_fix.current: Nav says 'NEWS', page heading and breadcrumb say 'BLOG'
suggested_fix.proposed: Use one name everywhere — change the page heading and breadcrumb to 'News' to match the nav item and the /news route

finding: There is no Contact link in the top navigation; the only way to reach /contact is the small footer link or knowing the URL.

suggested_fix.current: Top nav: About, Resources, Case Studies, News (no Contact)
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a 'Contact' link to the top navigation

finding: Form errors rely solely on native browser bubbles ('Please fill out this field.') with no programmatic error summary and no in-page, persistent, screen-reader-announced error list. The exploration log records 'no visible validation errors after empty submit' for the custom layer.

suggested_fix.current: Native-only required validation, transient bubbles, no error summary
suggested_fix.proposed: Add aria-invalid + aria-describedby per field with persistent inline messages, and an aria-live='assertive' error summary at the top of the form listing each failed field with anchor links

finding: A transient upstream failure on a Webflow-CDN news thumbnail can surface as a broken image because NewsCard's next/image has no onError/fallback; the host is already whitelisted and the proxy now returns 200, so this is a graceful-degradation gap, not a config blocker.

suggested_fix.current: next/image proxy returns 500 for the cdn.prod.website-files.com source; card renders broken with alt text
suggested_fix.proposed: Whitelist the CDN host in next.config images.remotePatterns (or migrate the asset into managed media), and add an onError/fallback image so a failed thumbnail degrades gracefully

finding: On first open of the page editor a 'Quick Tour' overlay sits on top of the outline panel it points at and blocks interaction until dismissed.

suggested_fix.current: Quick Tour overlay obscures the panel/button it references
suggested_fix.proposed: Position the tour tooltip beside (not over) its target, and let the user dismiss or skip the whole tour with one obvious button

finding: The Redirects manager is an orphan route with zero inbound links from any explored admin page.

suggested_fix.current: /admin/pages/redirects reachable only by URL
suggested_fix.proposed: Link 'Redirects' from the SEO pages screen header and/or the studio sidebar

finding: The primary nav CTA 'STEP INSIDE' does not communicate what it does (it links to /apply).

suggested_fix.current: Nav button label 'STEP INSIDE'
suggested_fix.proposed: 'APPLY NOW' (match the hero CTA so the primary action is named consistently)

finding: On first load the article cards render as blank outlines until the user scrolls (scroll-reveal animation), so the page looks broken or half-loaded above the fold.

suggested_fix.current: Article cards are invisible until scrolled into view
suggested_fix.proposed: Render the first row of article cards visible on load; only apply reveal animation to cards genuinely below the fold

finding: No News/Blog item in the studio sidebar; /admin/news is reachable only by typing the URL.

suggested_fix.current: Sidebar: SEO pages, Media library, Settings only
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a 'Blog & news' sidebar entry linking to /admin/news alongside SEO pages

finding: Serious color-contrast failure on the homepage statistic values (the 'definition' values under the Accelerator Program). Three <dd> elements fail WCAG AA contrast.

suggested_fix.current: Stat label/value color below 4.5:1 against background
suggested_fix.proposed: Darken the <dd> text (or lighten background) to meet at least 4.5:1 contrast

finding: Legacy /blog/{slug} paths hard-404 instead of redirecting to the live /news/{slug} article that exists.

suggested_fix.current: /blog/{slug} returns 404
suggested_fix.proposed: 301-redirect /blog/{slug} to the corresponding /news/{slug} article

finding: The first paint of /news shows article cards as empty outlines (scroll-reveal animation) and one card renders as broken-image alt text instead of an image ('Top 10 Profitable Products to Stock in Your Vending Machine (Snacks, Healthy Items, Niche Products)').

suggested_fix.current: Scroll-reveal cards paint empty; one image returns 500 from /_next/image (legacy website-files.com CDN)
suggested_fix.proposed: Render card content visible by default (reveal as enhancement only) and fix/replace the failing website-files.com image source so /_next/image returns 200

finding: The hero stat counters render as zeroes in the captured state: '0+' Entrepreneurs launched, '$0M+' Snack/Drink sales, '0+' Vending locations. These are count-up animations frozen at their start value, so the most prominent proof numbers on the homepage read as literal zero.

suggested_fix.current: Counters animate from 0 with no static fallback, so first paint and reduced-motion show '0+ / $0M+ / 0+'
suggested_fix.proposed: Render the final value as the default content and only animate as a progressive enhancement; respect prefers-reduced-motion by showing the final number immediately

finding: The three headline statistics render as '0+', '$0M+', '0+' instead of real figures, making the site look broken and untrustworthy.

suggested_fix.current: Counters show '0+', '$0M+', '0+'
suggested_fix.proposed: Render the real counts (e.g. '500+', '$12M+', '1,200+'); if a count is unknown at load, hide the stat rather than show 0

finding: Submitting the apply form with empty required fields produces NO visible validation error; the form silently does nothing. A non-native user has no message to read and cannot tell what is wrong.

suggested_fix.current: No validation message on empty/invalid submit
suggested_fix.proposed: Show a specific inline error per field, e.g. "Email is required" / "Enter a valid email address like name@example.com".

finding: Serious aria-prohibited-attr violation in the admin news list (.text-slate-300) — an ARIA attribute is applied to an element whose role does not allow it.

suggested_fix.current: Prohibited ARIA attribute (e.g. aria-label on a non-interactive/incorrect role element)
suggested_fix.proposed: Remove the disallowed ARIA attribute or wrap the content in an element with a role that permits it

finding: The homepage has a Cumulative Layout Shift of 0.263, well above the 0.1 'good' threshold; the same 0.263 CLS recurs on /about, /news, and every news article page, indicating a shared layout component shifts after load.

suggested_fix.current: CLS 0.263 on /, /about, /news, and article pages
suggested_fix.proposed: Reserve space for the shifting element (explicit width/height on hero/logo images, font-display swap with metric-matched fallback, fixed-height header) to bring CLS under 0.1

finding: A 'Schedule failed' filter is exposed in the workflow navigation with no inline explanation of what failed or how to recover.

suggested_fix.current: 'Schedule failed' filter link with no context
suggested_fix.proposed: When the filter has results, show what failed and a Retry action per item; hide or de-emphasise the filter when there are zero failures

finding: A 'Quick Tour · Step 1 of 3' overlay covers the outline panel on first editor open and blocks interaction until dismissed.

suggested_fix.current: Modal tour overlay auto-opens over the builder panel and blocks clicks
suggested_fix.proposed: Make the tour an opt-in '?' button; never auto-block the panel — or remember dismissal per-user so it shows at most once

finding: Hero stat counters render as '0+', '$0M+', '0+' placeholders.

suggested_fix.current: 0+ / $0M+ / 0+ stat values
suggested_fix.proposed: Populate real figures, or remove the stat band until real numbers exist — never ship placeholder zeros

finding: The homepage has horizontal scroll at mobile-375, making the landing page feel broken on the phone a social-media visitor arrives with.

suggested_fix.current: Homepage content overflows viewport width at 375px (horizontal scroll)
suggested_fix.proposed: Constrain offending element(s) with max-width:100% / overflow-x clipping so the page fits the 375px viewport

finding: No skip-to-content link. The first tab stop is the logo, followed by all primary nav links and (on the homepage) 12 video tab stops, before any main content or the Apply CTA in the body is reachable.

suggested_fix.current: No skip link; first focusable element is the logo
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a visually-hidden 'Skip to main content' link as the first focusable element that targets <main> and becomes visible on focus

finding: Serious color-contrast failures on the 404 page: the '404' label (.tracking-wide, light blue on white) and the explanatory subtext (.mt-2, light gray on white) both fail AA.

suggested_fix.current: Light-blue '404' and light-gray subtext below 4.5:1
suggested_fix.proposed: Use darker, AA-compliant colors for the '404' label and the subtext

finding: Serious color-contrast failure on article meta text (.mt-5, the date / '13 min read' line) and, on sibling articles, on inline body links (p > a). Article body links fail AA.

suggested_fix.current: Faint gray meta text and low-contrast inline link color
suggested_fix.proposed: Raise meta and inline-link colors to meet 4.5:1; underline inline links so they are distinguishable without relying on color

finding: Serious color-contrast failures on the media library filter counts (.mr-1) and a description paragraph (.space-y-2 > p), plus a broken heading order (h3 without preceding h2).

suggested_fix.current: Faint gray filter counts/description text below 4.5:1; h3 with no h2
suggested_fix.proposed: Darken the count and description text to AA; fix the heading hierarchy so the section h2 precedes the h3

finding: The successful application submission is announced only as a single line of green text beside the submit button, with an empty <alert> region present in the DOM but no confirmed live announcement; no redirect to the existing /thank-you-for-applying page.

suggested_fix.current: Subtle inline green text; empty/unconfirmed alert region; no redirect
suggested_fix.proposed: Announce success via a populated aria-live='polite' region (or move focus to a success heading) and/or redirect to /thank-you-for-applying

finding: After a successful application the only confirmation is a small line of green text beside the button: "Thanks. We received your details and will follow up shortly." For a non-native reader this is easy to miss and the phrase "follow up" is a phrasal verb that is ambiguous (will they email me? call me? when?).

suggested_fix.current: Thanks. We received your details and will follow up shortly.
suggested_fix.proposed: Redirect to /thank-you-for-applying, or show a large confirmation: "Application received. We will email you within 2 business days."

finding: The nav item, the URL and the page heading use three different words for the same section: nav says "NEWS", the route is /news, but the page heading is "BLOG" with the eyebrow "Insights from the route". A non-native reader cannot tell whether News and Blog are the same place.

suggested_fix.current: nav "NEWS" -> page heading "BLOG"
suggested_fix.proposed: Use one word everywhere: heading "News" to match the nav item and /news route.

finding: No price, price range, or explanation of how pricing works appears anywhere on the public site; the program asks for an application with the cost entirely hidden.

suggested_fix.current: No pricing anywhere; cost revealed only after an application/call
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a Pricing or 'What it costs' section (a 'from $X' figure, a price band, or at minimum 'investment discussed on your call, typically $X-$Y range, no obligation')

finding: The hero and supporting copy lean on 'financial freedom' and 'passive income' with 'minimal time investment' — the exact framing of get-rich-quick funnels that a skeptical buyer is trained to distrust.

suggested_fix.current: Turn Vending Into Your Path to Financial Freedom … with minimal time investment
suggested_fix.proposed: Lead with a concrete, defensible promise (e.g. 'Learn the exact system to place your first profitable vending machine in 90 days') and drop 'minimal time investment'

finding: A specific income figure ('making $10,000 a month in vending machines') is quoted approvingly inside a homepage testimonial with no earnings disclaimer anywhere near it.

suggested_fix.current: $10,000/month figure in a testimonial, no disclaimer nearby
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a visible earnings disclaimer adjacent to any income claim ('Individual results vary; this is not a guarantee of income') and link it to the Terms income-disclaimer section

finding: The founder is presented by first name only ('Mike') with no surname, credentials, company registration, or verifiable identity.

suggested_fix.current: Founder identified only as 'Mike'
suggested_fix.proposed: Use his full name, a one-line verifiable bio, and link to a LinkedIn/business profile and the registered company entity (Modern Amenities) named in the legal pages

finding: The article H1 overflows the right edge of a 375px phone — the long uppercase word 'ENTREPRENEURSHIP' is not breaking/wrapping, so it runs off-screen and the page scrolls horizontally.

suggested_fix.current: Article H1 with no word-break on long words; overflows at 375px
suggested_fix.proposed: Add overflow-wrap:anywhere / hyphens:auto (or clamp font-size with clamp()) to the article H1 so long words break within the viewport

finding: The homepage scrolls horizontally on a 375px phone. Above the fold renders fine (hamburger nav, stacked hero), so the overflow comes from content further down the page.

suggested_fix.current: A below-fold section on / exceeds the 375px viewport width
suggested_fix.proposed: Audit home sections for fixed widths / negative margins / oversized images and constrain with max-w-full + overflow-x-hidden on the page wrapper

finding: The /case-studies page scrolls horizontally on a 375px phone. The header area fits; overflow is below the fold.

suggested_fix.current: A case-study card/quote section exceeds 375px width
suggested_fix.proposed: Constrain the case-study cards/testimonial rows to max-w-full and verify no fixed-width child forces overflow at 375px

finding: The public marketing site and the admin studio are two unrelated design systems. The public site uses heavy black display type, hard black borders, offset drop shadows, and orange CTAs (neo-brutalist); the admin studio uses a soft light-gray SaaS dashboard with rounded cards, hairline borders, and blue primaries. There is no shared component language, type scale, button shape, or colour token between the two halves of the product.

suggested_fix.current: Two independent visual languages (brutalist public, generic SaaS admin) with no shared tokens
suggested_fix.proposed: Define one token set (type scale, radius scale, brand orange/blue, shadow scale) and apply brand accents to the studio (e.g. orange primary actions, the brand display face on section headings) so the studio reads as the same product

finding: Nearly every public page reports a Cumulative Layout Shift of 0.263 (home, about, news, all three articles, privacy-free pages excepted). A CLS of 0.263 is well above Google's 0.1 'good' threshold and indicates a consistent, repeatable jump as the page settles.

suggested_fix.current: CLS 0.263 on most public routes from a shared above-fold element / font swap
suggested_fix.proposed: Reserve space for the shifting element (explicit hero image dimensions) and use next/font with font-display:optional or a size-adjust fallback to remove the swap-driven reflow

finding: The 404 page is the only public page that abandons the brand. 'Page not found' is set in a rounded system sans-serif at a modest size with a thin outline 'Back to home' pill, sitting inside the otherwise-branded header/footer. Every other public page uses the heavy black display face and the orange/black brand button.

suggested_fix.current: Default sans 'Page not found' heading + thin outline 'Back to home' pill
suggested_fix.proposed: Set the 404 heading in the brand display face (e.g. 'PAGE NOT FOUND.') and use the standard orange brand button for 'Back to home' so it matches the rest of the site

finding: On the New blog post page, 'Save draft' and 'Publish' are rendered as two visually identical full-width blue buttons stacked directly on top of each other. Publish (irreversible, makes content public) and Save draft (safe, private) carry the same colour, size, and weight, so the destructive-leaning action has no visual distinction.

suggested_fix.current: 'Save draft' and 'Publish' are identical full-width blue buttons
suggested_fix.proposed: Make 'Save draft' a secondary/outline button and 'Publish' the single solid primary, so the consequential action is visually distinct

finding: The header's only call-to-action button is labelled "STEP INSIDE" and links to /apply, but nothing says it leads to the application. The clear "APPLY NOW" wording only appears further down the page in the body.

suggested_fix.current: Header button text: "STEP INSIDE" → /apply
suggested_fix.proposed: "APPLY NOW" → /apply (keep one consistent apply label everywhere)

finding: The contact page offers only a form — no phone number, no email address, no business hours. A pragmatist who wants to call cannot.

suggested_fix.current: Contact page: form only
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a visible phone number, email address, and business hours above the form

finding: Nowhere on the public site is there any price, cost range, or fee for the Vending Accelerator Program. The only path to find out what it costs is to apply and get a sales call.

suggested_fix.current: No pricing anywhere; cost only revealed after applying
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a pricing or "what it costs / what's included" section (even a starting-from range) so a visitor can self-qualify before applying

finding: A dedicated /thank-you-for-applying confirmation page exists but the apply form never routes to it

suggested_fix.current: Apply form shows inline text and stays on /apply
suggested_fix.proposed: On successful submit, redirect to /thank-you-for-applying

finding: No progress saving on the apply form — an interruption that reloads the tab loses all entered data

suggested_fix.current: Uncontrolled single-shot form with no persistence
suggested_fix.proposed: Persist field values to localStorage as the user types and restore them on reload

finding: Legacy /blog/* article paths 404 with a bare, unstyled 'Not found' page that has no header, no footer, and no recovery link — a completely different 404 handler from the styled site-wide 404. Articles previously lived at /blog and the app links the public article page heading as 'BLOG'.

suggested_fix.current: /blog/{slug} returns a bare 'Not found' page with no nav/footer/recovery link
suggested_fix.proposed: Add 301 redirects from /blog/{slug} to /news/{slug}, or route the /blog subtree through the global styled not-found page with a 'Back to home' link

finding: A clear, well-designed application confirmation page exists but the apply form never routes to it; the real flow instead shows buried inline text.

suggested_fix.current: Apply form shows inline green text; /thank-you-for-applying is never reached
suggested_fix.proposed: Route successful applications to /thank-you-for-applying

finding: The news list rows offer no delete/archive affordance; archiving requires opening each post's editor.

suggested_fix.current: No row-level delete/archive in /admin/news; editor-only
suggested_fix.proposed: Add row actions (Edit / Archive / Delete) and bulk select to the news list

finding: The contact form has no visibly designed success or error region, carrying the same vanishing-confirmation risk seen on /apply.

suggested_fix.current: No prominent success/error state visible on the contact form
suggested_fix.proposed: Show a clear, prominent success confirmation (and a clear inline error on failure that preserves entered data) after submit

finding: The 404 page offers only a 'Back to home' link — no search, no contact/help link, and no suggestion of where content moved.

suggested_fix.current: 404 with only 'Back to home'
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a search box and links to News, Apply, and Contact on the 404 page

finding: The homepage produces a horizontal scrollbar at mobile width.

suggested_fix.current: Page content exceeds viewport width at 375px causing horizontal scroll
suggested_fix.proposed: Constrain full-bleed sections to 100vw / add overflow-x guards so the page fits the screen width

finding: Homepage layout shift on load is high (CLS 0.263) — content jumps as the page settles.

suggested_fix.current: CLS 0.263 on homepage load
suggested_fix.proposed: Reserve space for hero image, logo strip, and stat row (explicit width/height or aspect-ratio) to bring CLS under 0.1

finding: A news card image fails with a 500 error from a leftover Webflow CDN URL (rendering raw alt text), and the page heading 'BLOG' contradicts the 'NEWS' navigation label and /news route.

suggested_fix.current: Broken image from cdn.prod.website-files.com (500); heading 'BLOG' vs nav 'NEWS'
suggested_fix.proposed: Re-host the article images on the app's own storage/CDN and fix the 500; rename the page heading to 'NEWS' (or rename the nav item) so the section has one consistent name
